Worms on Singapore shores: text index

worms photo index worms in general Worms on Singapore shoresDon’t know the name of the worm? Try the worm photo index Intertidal and marine worms

tpg

Coeloplana sp.
Leathery soft coral ctenophores                                                          

in red are those listed among the threatened animals of Singapore from Davison, G.W. H. and P. K. L. Ng and Ho Hua Chew, 2008. The Singapore Red Data Book: Threatened plants and animals of Singapore.

*Location in italics indicates sightings shared by others.

Species are difficult to positively identify without close examination.
On this website, they are grouped by external features for convenience of display.*

Mangroves

sbw: sungei buloh wetland reserve including kranji mangroves, lck: lim chu kang

Northern shores

pgl: punggol, prp: pasir ris park, chg: changi, cj: chek jawa including pulau ubin, skd: pulau sekudu, bb: beting bronok,
tus: tuas, ecp: east coast park, tm: tanah merah.

Southern shores

lab: labrador + berlayar creek, snt: sentosa, cyr: cyrene reef, kus: kusu island, laz: lazarus island, sji: st. john’s island, sis: sisters islands, jng: pulau jong, tkr:pulau tekukor, hnt: pulau hantu, tpg: terumbus pempang darat, tengah and laut, smk: pulau semakau, tsm: submerged reefs off semakau - terumbu raya, beting bemban besar, terumbu bemban, terumbu semakau, rfl: raffles lighthouse, lfa: live firing area (Salu, Sudong, Pawai, Senang, Biola and submerged reefs in the area)
